reforms.description: Portugal has established a unit (U-TAX) to monitor and assess tax benefits, which published an assessment of the main tax expenditures in June 2025. Additionally, a tax incentive for scientific research and innovation (IFICI) was introduced in 2024, offering personal income tax cuts to highly qualified professionals, entrepreneurs, innovators, directors of approved start-ups, and academic researchers who become Portuguese taxpayers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a monitoring unit and tax incentives is expected to enhance innovation capacity and competitiveness, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentive for scientific research and innovation was introduced in 2024 and the monitoring unit has published assessments.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant changes to the tax system and incentives for innovation, but do not constitute a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The tax incentive for scientific research and innovation (IFICI) introduced in 2024 offers PIT tax cuts to highly qualified professionals, entrepreneurs, innovators, directors of approved start-ups, and academic researchers who become Portuguese taxpayers.

reforms.description: Portugal is increasing direct R&D support targeted at SMEs to enhance their innovation capacity and competitiveness.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D support for SMEs is expected to boost their innovation capacity, leading to enhanced competitiveness and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently announced and not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in R&D support for SMEs is a significant but not transformative change in funding policy.
reforms.source_quote: Increasing direct R&D support targeted at SMEs.

reforms.description: The Recovery and Resilience Plan includes the design of additional vocational education and training (VET) programmes focusing on adult literacy, with a budget of EUR 225 million to support lifelong learning. Additionally, Portugal is investing EUR 710 million to upgrade VET facilities, aiming to modernize programs and improve the school-to-work transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in vocational education and training is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby supporting economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The VET programmes and facility upgrades are part of the Recovery and Resilience Plan and have been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant investments in education and training but do not constitute a major overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The planned design of additional VET programmes that focus on adult literacy and the upscaling of the National Plan for Adult Literacy, including more projects and financial support for equipment, staff and participants, worth EUR 225 million are helping to support lifelong learning.

reforms.description: Portugal's government launched the Decent Work Agenda in 2023, which includes measures to improve working conditions and reduce labour market segmentation, particularly for vulnerable groups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving working conditions and reducing labour market segmentation is expected to enhance productivity and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Decent Work Agenda was launched in 2023 and includes various measures that have been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Decent Work Agenda represents a major reform in labour policy aimed at improving working conditions.
reforms.source_quote: The Portuguese Government launched in 2023 a Decent Work Agenda (Agenda do Trabalho Digno), containing around 70 measures to improve working conditions.

reforms.description: The 'Mais Habitação' programme includes measures such as reducing personal income tax on long-term rentals and exempting rental income for owners switching to long-term rentals, aimed at promoting affordable housing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promoting affordable housing through tax reductions is expected to enhance living conditions and support economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures under the 'Mais Habitação' programme have been implemented as of 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The 'Mais Habitação' programme represents a significant reform in housing policy aimed at improving affordability.
reforms.source_quote: In October 2023 'Mais Habitação' programme was passed. Main measures include: the personal income tax rate (PIT) on income from long-term rentals was reduced; rental income until 2029 for owners switching from short- to long-term rentals was fully exempted from PIT.

reforms.description: The 'Construir Portugal' programme aims to simplify procedures for transferring rural land to urban use for affordable housing, including tax exemptions for first-time buyers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying land transfer procedures and providing tax exemptions is expected to facilitate housing development and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The 'Construir Portugal' programme has been implemented as of 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The 'Construir Portugal' programme represents a major reform in land use policy aimed at addressing housing challenges.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2024 the 'Construir Portugal' programme was passed. Main measures include: simplification of procedures to transfer rural to urban land for construction of residential properties with at least 70% of the construction area being allocated to social or affordable housing.

reforms.description: Portugal's Recovery and Resilience Plan (RRP) funding of over EUR 1.2 billion is expected to provide housing for around 26,000 households by 2026 through construction and purchasing and renovating existing dwellings.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The significant funding for housing is expected to improve living conditions and stimulate economic activity in the construction sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding is part of the Recovery and Resilience Plan and is expected to be implemented by 2026.
reforms.importance_rationale: The RRP funding represents a major investment in housing, addressing a critical societal need.
reforms.source_quote: Recovery and Resilience Plan (RRP) funding of over EUR 1.2 billion is expected to provide housing for around 26,000 households by 2026 through construction and purchasing and renovating existing dwellings.

reforms.description: The government is planning to introduce a renovation passport that provides a tailored roadmap for improving energy performance in buildings, complementing existing energy performance certificates. Additionally, the establishment of one-stop-shops is proposed to streamline access to information and services related to energy renovations, making it easier for homeowners to navigate available support.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to information and support for energy renovations is expected to enhance energy efficiency and contribute to sustainability goals.
reforms.status_evidence: The renovation passport and one-stop-shop proposals are currently announced and not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed measures represent significant improvements in energy renovation support but do not constitute a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Establishing one-stop-shops would make information more easily accessible and help streamline renovations.

reforms.description: The government aims to expand targeted financial support for energy renovations, particularly for low-income households, to address high upfront costs and promote deep renovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding financial support for energy renovations is expected to alleviate upfront costs for low-income households, promoting energy efficiency and sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans to expand financial support for energy renovations.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of financial support for energy renovations is a significant but not transformative change.
reforms.source_quote: Financial support for renovations has been low... the government is currently devising new programmes.
